title: Package Hardening V1
objective: Advance admitted Inventory, Persistence, Settings/Accessibility, and Semantic Interaction families only through package-specific compatibility, migration, and clean-consumer evidence.
scope: Independent family hardening checkpoints and one maintainer-controlled evidence integration checkpoint
non_goals: No new system admission, cross-family evidence inheritance, or renderer, Android, XR, or device claim without its separate gate

checkpoints.title: Harden the Inventory family at its earliest unsatisfied gates
checkpoints.outcome: Each Inventory module earns current-revision tests or clean-consumer evidence independently, without inheriting proof from sibling modules or earlier tuples.
non_goals: No all-module promotion, visual polish, Android build, or named-device claim

required_capabilities: Can implement Unity package tests and reproduce immutable Git-consumer dependency tuples
required_tools: Git, Python 3, a supported Unity Editor for Unity checkpoints, and repository validator
qualification_evidence: Commit-bound module-specific tests or clean-consumer receipts matching the selected Inventory gate

acceptance.criterion: The selected module closes its documented earliest unsatisfied gate at an immutable commit and exact dependency tuple.
Evidence is not inherited across Core, Unity, Presentation, renderer, XR adapter, version, or environment boundaries.
Any remaining module, Android, renderer, or device gate stays explicit after the checkpoint.

verification.procedure: Run the selected module tests or clean-consumer profile and the full repository validator at the evidence commit.
verification.expected: The exact selected gate passes and unrelated maturity claims remain unchanged.

checkpoints.exact_next_action: Comment /claim PH-01 on issue #10, name one Inventory module gate, and publish a checkpoint-scoped branch before changing it.

checkpoints.title: Harden Persistence through compatibility and upgrade evidence
checkpoints.outcome: Persistence has a reviewed public API boundary, immutable clean consumer, deterministic upgrade and rollback exercise, and explicit unsupported-data behavior.
non_goals: No project save format, cloud backend, renderer, or device claim
do_not_touch: Product-owned save data or backend credentials

required_capabilities: Can review persistence APIs and test deterministic migration, recovery, and rollback behavior
required_tools: Git, Python 3, a supported Unity Editor for Unity evidence, and repository validator
qualification_evidence: Immutable clean-consumer and upgrade or rollback receipts for the Persistence tuple

acceptance.criterion: Public API and compatibility policy are reviewed without silently promising stability.
One exact consumer validates upgrade and rollback while preserving deterministic valid data.
Unsupported, corrupted, or future-version data behavior is explicit and tested.

verification.procedure: Reproduce the clean consumer, upgrade, rollback, and failure-path suite at the recorded commit.
verification.expected: The exact Persistence tuple passes and no unrelated family or device claim changes.

checkpoints.exact_next_action: Comment /claim PH-02 on issue #70 and publish a bounded branch for the Persistence compatibility and migration evidence.

checkpoints.title: Harden Settings and Accessibility through compatibility and migration evidence
checkpoints.outcome: Settings and Accessibility preserve stable identities, defaults, valid user choices, deterministic migration, and explicit invalid or removed-setting behavior.
non_goals: No product settings menu, visual design, or named-device accessibility claim
do_not_touch: Product copy, visual layout, and device support

required_capabilities: Can review settings and accessibility metadata APIs and test deterministic migration and rollback
required_tools: Git, Python 3, a supported Unity Editor for Unity evidence, and repository validator
qualification_evidence: Immutable clean-consumer and user-choice migration receipts for the Settings tuple

acceptance.criterion: Identifiers, defaults, validation, discoverability metadata, and compatibility policy are reviewed.
One exact consumer validates upgrade and rollback without losing valid user choices.
Unknown, invalid, removed, and future settings follow explicit tested behavior.

verification.procedure: Reproduce the clean consumer, migration, rollback, and invalid-setting suite at the recorded commit.
verification.expected: The exact Settings tuple passes and renderer or device claims remain unchanged.

checkpoints.exact_next_action: Comment /claim PH-03 on issue #71 and publish a bounded branch for Settings compatibility and migration evidence.

checkpoints.title: Harden Semantic Interaction through compatibility and adapter evidence
checkpoints.outcome: Semantic intents, capabilities, state, policy, and Unity adapters preserve their engine-light boundary through compatibility and upgrade evidence.
non_goals: No complete XR interaction design system, vendor controller mapping, or device claim

required_capabilities: Can review semantic interaction contracts and test deterministic adapter and migration behavior
required_tools: Git, Python 3, a supported Unity Editor for Unity evidence, and repository validator
qualification_evidence: Immutable clean-consumer and adapter-boundary receipts for the Interaction tuple

acceptance.criterion: Intent, capability, context, state, policy, and adapter APIs receive a bounded compatibility review.
One exact consumer validates deterministic upgrade and rollback behavior.
Core and Unity layers remain independent of renderer, input vendor, and named device.

verification.procedure: Reproduce the clean consumer, adapter-boundary, upgrade, and rollback suite at the recorded commit.
verification.expected: The exact Interaction tuple passes and no live interaction or device claim is inferred.

checkpoints.exact_next_action: Comment /claim PH-04 on issue #72 and publish a bounded branch for Interaction compatibility and adapter evidence.

checkpoints.title: Independently review and integrate Package Hardening results
checkpoints.outcome: Only family results whose exact commit and dependency tuple satisfy declared gates update catalogs, roadmap, maturity, and release notes.
non_goals: No all-or-nothing batch promotion, self-review, or evidence inheritance
do_not_touch: Repository permissions and unsupported compatibility claims

required_capabilities: Can independently reproduce package evidence and review compatibility, maturity, migration, and release boundaries
required_tools: Git, Python 3, declared Unity evidence environments, and repository validator
qualification_evidence: Independent exact-commit review of each family proposed for integration

acceptance.criterion: Each family result is accepted or deferred independently at its exact evidence commit.
Catalog, compatibility profiles, roadmap, release notes, and maturity agree without broadening claims.
Full validation, CI, independent review, and maintainer promotion decisions pass.

verification.procedure: Reproduce the commands and evidence for every family selected for integration, then run the full repository contract.
verification.expected: Only supported family claims change and all repository checks pass.

checkpoints.exact_next_action: Wait for all non-cancelled family checkpoints to complete, then request the PH-INT integration lease in issue #78.

integration.strategy: The maintainer independently reviews and integrates each family whose exact evidence satisfies its declared gates; incomplete families remain unchanged.
